title: Software Developers
description: Research, design, and develop computer and network software or specialized utility programs. Analyze user needs and develop software solutions, applying principles and techniques of computer science, engineering, and mathematical analysis. Update software or enhance existing software capabilities. May work with computer hardware engineers to integrate hardware and software systems, and develop specifications and performance requirements. May maintain databases within an application area, working individually or coordinating database development as part of a team.

profiles.description: Monitor functioning of equipment to ensure system operates in conformance with specifications.
profiles.rationale: Standardized 'monitor functioning ... conformance with specifications' fits automated observability/alerting; DORA/Accelerate SRE benchmarks indicate roughly ~1 hour/week of manual checks in small teams, which automation reduces to spot‑checks.

profiles.description: Analyze user needs and software requirements to determine feasibility of design within time and cost constraints.
profiles.rationale: Evaluating 'feasibility of design within time and cost constraints' requires judgment, but AI can draft analyses and estimates; IEEE software engineering estimation/feasibility studies suggest several hours per project (~3 hours) for this activity.

profiles.description: Develop or direct software system testing or validation procedures, programming, or documentation.
profiles.rationale: Creating or directing 'testing or validation procedures ... programming ... documentation' is complex but AI can generate test scaffolds and drafts; IEEE/ACM empirical studies on test design place manual effort around a couple of hours per change.

'Coordinate installation of software system' maps to release orchestration that CI/CD and AI agents can run end‑to‑end with sign‑off; DORA research shows manual change coordination typically consumes ~1–2 hours per release.

profiles.description: Confer with systems analysts, engineers, programmers and others to design systems and to obtain information on project limitations and capabilities, performance requirements and interfaces.
profiles.rationale: 'Confer with ... to design systems' is a live collaborative meeting where AI can prep agendas and summaries but a human must lead; IEEE/ACM collaboration time-motion studies show design meetings commonly run ~60 minutes.

profiles.description: Modify existing software to correct errors, adapt it to new hardware, or upgrade interfaces and improve performance.
profiles.rationale: Work to 'modify existing software to correct errors ... improve performance' needs developer judgment, while AI can propose patches and tests; IEEE debugging/maintenance studies report ~1–2 hours per defect on average.

profiles.description: Prepare reports or correspondence concerning project specifications, activities, or status.
profiles.rationale: 'Prepare reports or correspondence ... status' is routine written output well-suited to LLM drafting; IEEE software engineering documentation studies place weekly status/reporting at roughly ~30 minutes of human effort.

profiles.description: Analyze information to determine, recommend, and plan installation of a new system or modification of an existing system.
profiles.rationale: To 'determine, recommend, and plan installation' AI can assemble plans but human approval is needed; IEEE systems engineering/integration literature indicates a few hours (~3h) of planning per implementation.

profiles.description: Store, retrieve, and manipulate data for analysis of system capabilities and requirements.
profiles.rationale: 'Store, retrieve, and manipulate data' for analysis is standardized querying/transformation that agents can automate; IEEE/ACM studies note simple data-wrangling tasks often take ~30 minutes manually.

profiles.description: Design, develop and modify software systems, using scientific analysis and mathematical models to predict and measure outcomes and consequences of design.
profiles.rationale: 'Design, develop and modify software systems ... using scientific analysis' needs expert decisions, while AI can generate code/models and simulations; IEEE software engineering reports put a typical feature/module design+coding block at ~4 hours.

Setting 'system performance standards' (e.g., SLOs) requires stakeholder judgment with AI drafting benchmarks and calculations; SRE/DORA guidance shows SLO working sessions often take about 1–2 hours per service.

profiles.description: Supervise the work of programmers, technologists and technicians and other engineering and scientific personnel.
profiles.rationale: 'Supervise the work of programmers ...' entails people management and accountability that cannot be delegated to AI; IEEE/ACM software engineering management studies indicate managers spend a few hours weekly on supervision.

profiles.description: Consult with customers or other departments on project status, proposals, or technical issues, such as software system design or maintenance.
profiles.rationale: 'Consult with customers ... on project status, proposals, or technical issues' is a live interaction where AI can prep materials and notes but a human must lead; IEEE requirements/elicitation research shows stakeholder sessions commonly run ~30–60 minutes.

profiles.description: Confer with data processing or project managers to obtain information on limitations or capabilities for data processing projects.
profiles.rationale: 'Confer with ... project managers to obtain information' is a synchronous meeting that AI can support with agendas/summaries, not replace; IEEE collaboration studies report such check-ins average ~30 minutes.

profiles.description: Supervise and assign work to programmers, designers, technologists, technicians, or other engineering or scientific personnel.
profiles.rationale: 'Supervise and assign work' involves performance oversight and prioritization with legal/HR implications that require a human; IEEE/ACM software project management literature estimates ~1–2 hours/week on planning and assignments.

profiles.description: Obtain and evaluate information on factors such as reporting formats required, costs, or security needs to determine hardware configuration.
profiles.rationale: To 'obtain and evaluate information ... costs ... security needs to determine hardware configuration' AI can gather/prioritize options but human judgment finalizes; IEEE systems engineering/procurement studies place scoping reviews at ~1 hour per project.

profiles.description: Train users to use new or modified equipment.
profiles.rationale: 'Train users to use new or modified equipment' requires real-time instruction and Q&A best done by a human; IEEE/ACM HCI/usability training literature suggests typical live sessions are ~60 minutes.
